introduction: A portrait film about the work of the Vanemuine ballet dancer Ülo Vilimaa by TallinnfilmÜlo Vilimaa is a Soviet and Estonian ballet dancer and choreographer.In 1962 he graduated from the Tallinn Ballet School, in 1964-65 he trained at the Leningrad Ballet School. Ballet soloist from 1962, chief choreographer of the Vanemuine Theater in Tartu from 1973. He developed Estonian ballet in line with classical traditions with the introduction of a special national flavor.In 1985 he was awarded the title of People's Artist of Estonia.Ülo Vilimaa, Juris Zhigurs, Vizma Vitol, Maye Saealle, corps de ballet of the Vanemuine TheaterIn a programme:Romeo and Juliet (S. Prokofiev)Paganini (S. Rachmaninov)Dance of Death (Sense-Sans)Kratt (E. Tubin)Collage (A. Pärt)Syllabic music (A. Pärt)
